hero.label: Who We Are
hero.title: About RAPP
hero.subtitle: We are Rwandans Allied for Peace and Progress — a movement rooted in hope, driven by community, and committed to lasting change.

story.label: Our Story
story.title: Born from Rwanda's Spirit of Resilience
paragraphs: RAPP was founded in 2003 in Kigali as a local non-governmental organization (NGO) and implements projects from different international donors.   RAPP works in urban and rural districts in collaboration with partners composed of Government and National and International NGO’s.  RAPP recognizes the link between HIV prevention and stigma-reduction and discrimination against people who are living with HIV, and taboos related to sexual relations.  RAPP creates awareness among young people about Sexual and Reproductive Health and Rights (SRHR), HIV/AIDS and how to prevent it using theater and other unique forms of message transportation.

Our outreach also benefits people that are infected or affected by HIV and other vulnerable and low income people financially.  RAPP helps to reduce poverty by using our purchasing power to initiate and/or support business and Income Generating Activities (IGA) run by local self-help groups.

values.title: Peace First
values.desc: We believe dialogue and reconciliation are the foundation of all sustainable progress.

values.title: Community-Led
values.desc: Every programme is designed with — not for — the communities we serve.

values.title: Pan-African Spirit
values.desc: We draw on Rwanda's rich heritage while embracing our shared African identity.

values.title: Accountability
values.desc: We hold ourselves to the highest standards of transparency and results-based management.

values.title: Sustainability
values.desc: We build programmes that communities can own and sustain long after our involvement.

values.title: Equity & Inclusion
values.desc: We prioritize the most marginalized — women, youth, and rural communities.

milestones.event: RAPP founded in Kigali by a coalition of peacebuilders and development practitioners.
Launched first reconciliation dialogue programme, reaching 20 communities.
Received first international grant from the EU to expand to the Northern Province.
Scaled Youth Leadership Academy to three provinces, training 300+ youth annually.
Celebrated impacting over 40,000 lives and partnering with 25+ organizations.
Launched the Reforestation Rwanda campaign with a goal of 1 million trees by 2027.
